title: About Me | Graham Stuart MP
headings: h2: Working Hard for Beverley & Holderness
h3: Graham Stuart MP for Beverley and Holderness
text_excerpt: About Me | Graham Stuart MP Skip to main content Graham Stuart MP Main navigation Home News Priorities My Views Street Surgeries About Me Contact Cost of Living Graham Stuart MP About Me Tweet What I've Done You can read about what I've done for Beverley and Holderness at www.grahamstuart.com/whativedone . Some Facts About Me I was born in 1962 in Carlisle, the son of a doctor. I was educated at Glenalmond College before reading Philosophy and Law at Selwyn College, Cambridge from 1982-1985. I started my publishing business at the end of my first year at Cambridge. I was elected to Cambridge City Council in 1998, and served there until 2004. I stood for Parliament in Cambridge in 2001 before moving to Beverley in 2004. I was first elected as Beverley and Holderness's Member of Parliament in 2005. Political Positions 1998-2004 Cambridge City Councillor, Cherry Hinton Ward 2005-Present Member of Parliament, Beverley and Holderness 2006-2010 Member, Conservative Party Board 2010-2015 Chairman, House of Commons Education Selection Committee 2016-2018 Government Whip 2018-2021 Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State for International Trade 2022 Prime Minister's Trade Envoy to Vietnam 2022 Minister of State for Europe 2022-2024 Minister of State for Energy Security and Net Zero Interests Football, Cricket, Rugby, cycling, climate change, motorcycling, rural affairs, punk rock. Working Hard for Beverley & Holderness Come and say hello to me at my informal street surgeries across the constituency. I also welcome calls, letters and emails to my office, the contact details for which can be found in the 'Contact' section at the top of this page. You can also sign up to my e-newsletter which will allow you to keep up to date with all the local campaigns I am involved in. In the meantime, keep visiting http://www.grahamstuart.com/news for all the latest news.
